title: Registries Configuration Schema
description: Schema for crucible's registries.json configuration file.  Defines the container registries used for the controller image, engine images (public and optional private), and optional external userenv image sources.

controller.description: Configuration for the controller container image registry.  The controller image contains all software needed to orchestrate benchmark runs.

url.description: The full URL of the controller container image (e.g., 'quay.io/org/controller').

tag.description: The image tag to pull.  Typically 'latest' for upstream installations.

pull-token.description: The path to a Docker/Podman auth JSON file for pulling the controller image, if authentication is required.

tls-verify.description: Whether TLS certificate verification is enabled when pulling the controller image.

engines.description: Configuration for engine container image registries.  Engine images contain benchmark and tool software.  A public registry is required; a private registry is optional for authenticated environments.

public.description: The public engine registry configuration.  This is the primary registry where engine images are pushed to and pulled from.

url.description: The full repository URL for the public engine registry.

push-token.description: The path to a Docker/Podman auth JSON file for pushing engine images to this registry.

tls-verify.description: Whether TLS certificate verification is enabled for this registry.

quay.description: Quay-specific configuration for managing image tag expirations.

private.description: An optional private engine registry configuration for environments that require authenticated image pulls.

url.description: The full repository URL for the private engine registry.

tokens.description: Authentication token file paths for push and pull operations on the private registry.

push.description: The path to a Docker/Podman auth JSON file for pushing images.

pull.description: The path to a Docker/Podman auth JSON file for pulling images.

tls-verify.description: Whether TLS certificate verification is enabled for this registry.

quay.description: Quay-specific configuration for managing image tag expirations.

userenvs.description: An optional array of external userenv image sources.  These registries provide additional base container images beyond the built-in userenvs.

items.description: An external userenv image source with its registry URL and authentication.

url.description: The registry URL where userenv images are stored.

pull-token.description: The path to a Docker/Podman auth JSON file for pulling images from this userenv registry.

tls-verify.description: Whether TLS certificate verification is enabled for this userenv registry.

quay.description: Configuration for Quay.io-specific image tag expiration management.  Quay automatically deletes images after their expiration period, so crucible can refresh expirations to prevent cached images from being deleted.

expiration-length.description: The duration for image tag expiration, specified as a number followed by 'w' (weeks) or 'd' (days).  For example, '13w' means 13 weeks.

refresh-expiration.description: Configuration for automatically refreshing image tag expirations via the Quay API.

token-file.description: The file path containing the OAuth refresh token for authenticating with the Quay API.

api-url.description: The Quay API URL used for expiration management operations.

require-success.description: Whether a successful image push is required before refreshing the expiration timer.  When true, only images that were actually pushed get their expirations refreshed.
